records.ety: [L. consore, -sortis; con- + sors lot, fate, share. See Sort.]

defs: One who shares the lot of another; a companion; a partner; especially, a wife or husband.
quotes: He single chose to live, and shunned to wed, Well pleased to want a consort of his bed.
The consort of the queen has passed from this troubled sphere.
The snow-white gander, invariably accompanied by his darker consort.

defs: A ship keeping company with another.

quotes: Take it singly, and it carries an air of levity; but, in consort with the rest, has a meaning quite different.

defs: An assembly or association of persons; a company; a group; a combination.
quotes: In one consort' there sat Cruel revenge and rancorous despite, Disloyal treason, and heart-burning hate.
Lord, place me in thy consort.

defs: Harmony of sounds; concert, as of musical instruments.
quotes: To make a sad consort'; Come, let us join our mournful song with theirs.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Consorted; p. pr. & vb. n. Consorting.]

defs: To unite or to keep company; to associate; -- used with with.
quotes: Which of the Grecian chiefs consorts with thee?

defs: To unite or join, as in affection, harmony, company, marriage, etc.; to associate.
quotes: For all that pleasing is to living ears Was there consorted in one harmony.
He begins to consort himself with men.
Thou, wretched boy, that didst consort him here, Shalt with him hence.
